{"credits": [{"type": "finder", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13433538?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/kerberosmansour", "id": 13433538, "login": "kerberosmansour", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjEzNDMzNTM4", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48032", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:H/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-697", "name": "Incorrect Comparison"}], "description": "**Affected:** `@hulumi/policies` `< 1.4.0` — **Fixed in:** `1.4.0` — **Severity:** High — **CWE-697 (Incorrect Comparison)**

#### Summary

AWS IAM trust policies can list more than one federated identity provider — for example, a role that accepts BOTH GitHub Actions OIDC and Google's OIDC. The `G_OIDC_1` and `G_OIDC_2` policy rules are supposed to flag IAM roles whose GitHub-OIDC trust is too permissive (e.g. wildcard `sub:` conditions that would let any branch or any pull request assume the role).

The bug: when the role's `Principal.Federated` field was a JSON array of multiple providers, the rules failed to recognise that GitHub Actions was one of them. The providers list was coerced into a single comma-joined string, the matcher only looked at the start, and the GitHub OIDC hostname was lost in the join. Both rules concluded "this isn't a GitHub-OIDC role" and skipped the wildcard check.

#### Impact

A trust policy that listed the real GitHub OIDC provider ARN alongside any second provider would slip past both detectors. Consumers using `HulumiHardeningPack` or `HulumiGithubHardeningPack` could ship an IAM role with wildcard `sub:` conditions (allowing untrusted PRs from forks to assume the role) while their policy validation reported the stack as compliant. The G_OIDC_2 detector also failed to mark such roles for the cluster-admin / `AdministratorAccess` blast-radius check.

#### Patches

Upgrade to `@hulumi/policies@1.4.0`. The shared GitHub-OIDC-provider matcher now correctly walks lists of providers — if any element of the list is the real GitHub OIDC ARN, the role is treated as GitHub-OIDC-assumable and the wildcard / blast-radius checks apply.

#### Workarounds

None reliable — upgrade is the fix.

#### Resources

- [PR #178](https://github.com/kerberosmansour/hulumi/pull/178) (Cluster A); regression tests at `packages/policies/tests/github/{g-oidc-2,github-oidc-issuer}.test.ts`.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0004, "percentile": 0.12464}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-g759-4pxw-6692", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-10T13:37:08Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-g759-4pxw-6692", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-g759-4pxw-6692"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48032"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-10T13:37:08Z", "references": ["https://github.com/kerberosmansour/hulumi/security/advisories/GHSA-g759-4pxw-6692", "https://github.com/kerberosmansour/hulumi/pull/178", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-g759-4pxw-6692"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/kerberosmansour/hulumi/security-advisories/GHSA-g759-4pxw-6692", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/kerberosmansour/hulumi", "summary": "@hulumi/policies bypasses IAM-role policy checks when the role trusts multiple OIDC providers", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-10T13:37:10Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-g759-4pxw-6692", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.4.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@hulumi/policies"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.4.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: @hulumi/policies bypasses IAM-role policy checks when the role trusts multiple OIDC providers
